agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform managing parcel delivery lifecycle — from shipment creation through transit to final delivery — with weight-based pricing brackets.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A parcel delivery unit tracking the movement of goods from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected to begin its journey."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is to be delivered."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a shipment after creation, before it begins transit."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"State indicating the shipment is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state indicating the shipment has reached its destination. No further state transitions allowed."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The mass of a shipment in kilograms. Must be positive and cannot exceed 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification determined by shipment weight: Standard, Heavy, or Freight."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments under 10 kg (weight < 10)."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments from 10 kg up to but not including 50 kg (10 ≤ weight < 50)."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments of 50 kg or more (weight ≥ 50)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is logically meaningless — it cannot represent a valid delivery intent. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Creating a shipment with a missing pickup address or missing delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions follow a strict forward-only lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Backward transitions (e.g., In Transit → Pending) would corrupt operational tracking and violate the physical reality of parcel movement.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to transition a shipment to a prior state must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Once a shipment reaches Delivered state, no further state changes are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivered is a terminal state reflecting a completed physical event. Allowing changes would corrupt delivery records and audit trails.","testable_assertion":"Any state transition attempt on a Delivered shipment must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly greater than zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would break pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight ≤ 0 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg cap is a business policy likely tied to fleet capacity or insurance limits. A VP could raise this limit with new equipment or partnerships.","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket Standard applies when weight < 10 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 9.99 kg must be classified as Standard."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket Heavy applies when 10 kg ≤ weight < 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 10 kg must be classified as Heavy; a shipment with weight 49.99 kg must be classified as Heavy."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Weight bracket Freight applies when weight ≥ 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight 50 kg must be classified as Freight; a shipment with weight 1000 kg must be classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user provides a valid pickup address, delivery address, and positive weight ≤ 1000 kg","when":"The user creates a shipment","then":"The shipment is created in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user omits the pickup address or the delivery address","when":"The user attempts to create a shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating the missing address"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A shipment exists in Pending state","when":"The shipment is moved to In Transit","then":"The shipment state becomes In Transit and the transition is recorded"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment exists in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to move it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment exists in Delivered state","when":"Any state change is attempted (to Pending or In Transit)","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A user provides a weight of 0, a negative weight, or a weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"The user attempts to create or update a shipment","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating invalid weight"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment is created with weight 9.99 kg","when":"The system assigns a weight bracket","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment is created with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The system assigns a weight bracket","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment is created with weight 49.99 kg","when":"The system assigns a weight bracket","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment is created with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The system assigns a weight bracket","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment is created with weight 1000 kg","when":"The system assigns a weight bracket","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary of 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (10 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of Heavy bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary of 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Freight (50 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of Freight bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at maximum allowed value of 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight"},{"scenario":"Weight of 0.01 kg (smallest meaningful positive weight)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Standard"},{"scenario":"Attempting to transition directly from Pending to Delivered (skipping In Transit)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not explicitly address this. Strict reading of the forward-only lifecycle (Pending → In Transit → Delivered) suggests skipping states should be rejected."},{"scenario":"Weight provided with excessive decimal precision (e.g., 9.999999999 kg)","expected_behavior":"System should apply consistent rounding or precision rules before bracket classification"},{"scenario":"Creating a shipment with empty-string addresses","expected_behavior":"Should be treated as missing — empty strings are not valid addresses"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket misclassification directly impacts revenue — undercharging Heavy as Standard or Freight as Heavy erodes margins. Correct boundary enforcement is critical to pricing integrity.","operational":"Strict state machine enforcement prevents operational chaos — parcels that revert to Pending after being In Transit would desynchronize driver manifests, warehouse operations, and tracking systems.","user":"Clear shipment lifecycle gives customers reliable tracking. Weight validation at creation prevents costly downstream corrections or failed pickups.","risk":"Primary risk is boundary precision errors in weight brackets causing systematic mis-pricing. Secondary risk is state machine bypass allowing data corruption in delivery records.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (shippers)","impact":"Accurate weight bracket classification determines shipping cost; clear state tracking provides delivery visibility."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Enforced state transitions ensure manifests and driver assignments remain consistent with actual parcel status."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight bracket accuracy directly determines revenue recognition and invoicing correctness."},{"group":"Warehouse staff","impact":"Shipment lifecycle states drive physical handling workflows — state integrity prevents processing errors."}]},"confidence":{"level":90,"reasoning":"The spec is clear, internally consistent, and covers state lifecycle, weight constraints, and pricing brackets with explicit boundary definitions. No contradictions detected. Minor gaps exist around edge cases not explicitly addressed (e.g., Pending→Delivered skip, decimal precision).","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions and terminal state rules","Weight validation bounds (positive, ≤ 1000 kg)","Weight bracket boundaries with explicit inclusive/exclusive definitions","Shipment creation prerequisites (both addresses required)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is permitted or forbidden — spec implies sequential but does not state it explicitly","Decimal precision handling for weights near bracket boundaries","Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation or only at creation time","Validation rules for address format (what constitutes a valid address beyond presence)"],"assumptions":["State transitions are sequential: Pending → In Transit → Delivered, with no state skipping allowed","Weight is immutable after creation or at least re-validated on any update","Empty or whitespace-only strings do not constitute valid addresses"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
